title: GTM AI Operator | UK (Growth Solutions Architect)
key_evidence: Core ask maps to Ryan's genuine spike: 'prototype and ship autonomous AI agents using Claude Code, n8n, AI Studio' for GTM workflows - this is exactly Ryan's Career-Break competitor-intel + growth-experiment agents

BUT hard reqs include 'shipping AI/LLM workflows IN PRODUCTION', SQL proficiency, Salesforce/Outreach/HubSpot RevOps stack - Ryan's agent work is self-directed/personal, not production-deployed in a revenue org; SQL not on CV
2-4 yrs RevOps/GTM Ops/Sales Ops required - Ryan's background is community-led growth/marketing, NOT classic RevOps; partial match
LinkedIn shows 'Over 200 applicants' at ~1 week old - very high competition; hot AI title at a hyped $17.3B / $1B ARR unicorn
Listed as 'Entry level' seniority on LinkedIn - large funnel, junior-friendly = flood of applicants
Deel is a massive org: dedicated recruiting/ATS keyword screening likely (SQL/Salesforce/RevOps keywords Ryan lacks) - high keyword-filter risk for non-traditional CV
Remote UK + clear structured process; salary likely >50k - good conversion realism IF callback
WTTJ mirror showed 'Job no longer available' but LinkedIn live ~1wk - role may be closing/refilling fast
biggest_lever: Lead the application with a concrete, demonstrable Claude Code agent artifact (the competitor-intel + growth-experiment agents) framed as production-style GTM automation - this is the one screen criterion where Ryan can genuinely out-evidence the 200+ applicants.
biggest_risk: ATS keyword filter at a 5,000+-person unicorn: missing SQL/Salesforce/RevOps/production-deployment keywords likely auto-screens a non-traditional CV before a human reads it; 200+ applicants make recruiter-read of a borderline CV unlikely.
apply_verdict: APPLY (with strong agent-portfolio cover) - lower-probability lottery ticket
